overrideNote: Historical version bumps in refactor commits accepted per user direction. All validators pass (test, typecheck, lint, repo-standards). Functional regressions fixed. DISABLE_VERSION_BUMP mechanism added for future commits.

blockingIssues.description: Commit 5f282b716e54da004e37c15d1e8091c68a4b5f77 adds a useful `DISABLE_VERSION_BUMP=1` escape hatch for future commits, but it does not create clean replacement commits for the original offending extension-core refactors. The historical version-bump scope violations in commits b2af1aaf5d8acde300b1b777930048db957615a0, 80a94959b2c822cd9cecb3360c65e50d10350fd5, a1c32b9bd31f6c722e58a02aa6bc96430e369731, 0f8b3ee4480885306977832351478b6313d2c387, and b2ce138f06ce8647606cdb940fd1963e01b9e0bd therefore remain unresolved.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the version-bump guidance to prefer `DISABLE_VERSION_BUMP=1 git commit ...` as the normal way to keep non-release commits clean after the hook change, and reserve `--no-verify` for explicit fallback scenarios where the hook itself is being repaired.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both round 3 review reports noted that repo AGENTS.md now documents `DISABLE_VERSION_BUMP=1`, and the passing `redo-ext-fixes-clean` feature relied on that flow successfully, but `.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md` still only instructs workers to use `git commit --no-verify` when version metadata changes appear.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Do not allow `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` unless the handoff explicitly records every required final validation step, or require a deviation entry for each skipped validator so shell/config-only work does not silently claim full procedural compliance.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The new `disable-version-bump-hook` handoff again marked `followedProcedure: true` while omitting several validations that `.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md` lists as mandatory, repeating the same compliance-reporting problem that round 2 already surfaced.
